title: Cramer's Rule
summary: Cramer's rule for solving n linear equations in n unknowns using determinants вАФ 2√Ч2 and 3√Ч3 examples including electric circuit current problems.
content_html:
1.7.3 Cramer's Rule

Cramer's rule solves AX = B where A is square (n√Чn) and det(A) вЙ† 0.

Theorem 1.1 вАФ Cramer's Rule:
The solution xi is given by:
xi = det(Ai) / det(A)
where Ai is A with its i-th column replaced by B.

Cramer's Rule for 2 Unknowns

AX = B:   aвВБx + bвВБy = cвВБ  and  aвВВx + bвВВy = cвВВ

x = |cвВБ,bвВБ; cвВВ,bвВВ| / |aвВБ,bвВБ; aвВВ,bвВВ|    y = |aвВБ,cвВБ; aвВВ,cвВВ| / |aвВБ,bвВБ; aвВВ,bвВВ|

Example 1.20 вАФ Electric circuit: 8iвВБ вИТ 3iвВВ = вИТ2 and вИТ3iвВБ + 10iвВВ = 6

det(A) = |8,вИТ3; вИТ3,10| = 80 вИТ 9 = 71
AвВБ = replace col 1 with B: |вИТ2,вИТ3; 6,10| = вИТ20+18 = вИТ2
AвВВ = replace col 2 with B: |8,вИТ2; вИТ3,6| = 48вИТ6 = 42
iвВБ = вИТ2/71 вЙИ вИТ0.028 A    iвВВ = 42/71 вЙИ 0.592 A

Cramer's Rule for 3 Unknowns

xi = det(Ai) / det(A)   for i = 1, 2, 3

Example 1.21 вАФ 3-mesh circuit:
10iвВБ вИТ 3iвВВ вИТ iвВГ = 3, вИТ3iвВБ + 14iвВВ вИТ 2iвВГ = вИТ6, вИТiвВБ вИТ 2iвВВ + 6iвВГ = 4

det(A) = |10,вИТ3,вИТ1; вИТ3,14,вИТ2; вИТ1,вИТ2,6| = 720
det(AвВБ) = |3,вИТ3,вИТ1; вИТ6,14,вИТ2; 4,вИТ2,6| = 200
det(AвВВ) = |10,3,вИТ1; вИТ3,вИТ6,вИТ2; вИТ1,4,6| = вИТ202
det(AвВГ) = |10,вИТ3,3; вИТ3,14,вИТ6; вИТ1,вИТ2,4| = 446
iвВБ
200/720 вЙИ 0.278 A
iвВВ
вИТ202/720 вЙИ вИТ0.281 A
iвВГ
446/720 вЙИ 0.619 A
When to use Cramer's rule: Works only for square, non-singular systems. For large n, Gaussian elimination is faster. Cramer's rule is elegant for 2√Ч2 and 3√Ч3 systems, especially in engineering applications (electric circuits, structural analysis).

Exercise Answers (1.3)

  • 2x+3y=8; 4x+5y=14 вЖТ x=1, y=2
  • 2xвИТy=5; 3x+2y=4 вЖТ x=2, y=вИТ1

exercises.title: Efficiency comparison

options_json: Much less efficient (requires many determinant calculations)
exercises.answer: Much less efficient (requires many determinant calculations)
